commit.message:
feat: refactor indexer as importable library

- Add public library API via src/lib/index.ts
- Export all services, types, and factory functions
- Configure package.json with exports field for library usage
- Add files field to control published files
- Add tsconfig.lib.json for library-specific build
- Create .npmignore to exclude source and dev files
- Refactor services to support both singleton and direct instantiation
- Make MetadataService constructor public
- Make ProcessorService accept options object
- Add BackfillConfig interface for configuration
- Update GeyserStreamingService with comprehensive JSDoc
- Add build:lib and build:all npm scripts
- Support dual-mode: standalone app and importable library

Library consumers can now:
import { GeyserStreamingService, MetadataService, ProcessorService } from 'solana-nft-indexer';
import type { Transaction, NFTMetadata } from 'solana-nft-indexer';

commit.message:
fix: use admin client for profile GET to bypass RLS

The profiles table RLS policy self-references, blocking regular
server client queries. Switch to getSupabaseAdmin() for the profile
GET endpoint to fix user icon and name not appearing in the nav bar.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix: handle Slack events in background to meet 3-second response limit

Slack requires HTTP 200 within 3 seconds. Moving event processing to
background task prevents timeout when Gemini/Slack API calls are slow.
Also creates fresh DB session for background task to avoid closed session issues.

commit.message:
feat: add JSON collapse compressor for large API/database responses

New compressor that intelligently collapses large JSON:
- Long arrays: show first 3 items + schema summary
- Homogeneous arrays: detect shared shape {keys}
- Deep nesting: collapse after depth 5
- Strip noise: remove null, empty strings, empty arrays

Auto-detected via classifier: text starting with { or [ that exceeds
maxTextTokens gets routed to json-collapse instead of dumb truncation.
Falls through to truncate if content is not valid JSON.

36 tests passing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Comprehensive design system: tokens, WCAG contrast, D3 chart rewrites

Design token system (chartTheme.ts):
- T.fg/label/muted/ghost: verified ≥4.5:1 contrast on #0A0A12
  (replaces 6 scattered grays: #404050, #505060, #606070, #8080A0, #9898A8)
- S.best/good/warn/bad/info: semantic status colors, unified red (#EF4444)
- FF.sans/mono, FS scale (nano=10, small=11, base=12, value=13, hero=26)
- axisLabel() helper for consistent axis formatting across all charts
- TOOLTIP_HEADER_STYLE, LEGEND_LABEL_STYLE — shared across all charts

Friction Scatter (FrictionScatterChart): D3 Canvas rewrite
- Unlimited point rendering (no SVG DOM limit) — handles 10k+ GPS points
- G-force ring overlays at 0.4, 0.8, 1.0, 1.2G (1.0G ring emphasized)
- Quadrant labels: Brake / Accel / Left / Right
- Heat coloring on single session (darker=slow, bright green=high G)
- Multi-session: tinted per session color

G-Force Envelope (FrictionCircleChart): Custom D3 SVG radar
- Replaces Recharts RadarChart (label overlap, limited control)
- Gradient fill per session, value dots on polygon vertices
- Numeric callouts per axis on single-session view
- Clean label placement, no overlap

All charts updated to use design tokens:
- LapTimesChart, CornerSpeedChart, CornerDetailTable
- WCAG fix: delta tile labels 7px→10px, table headers 8px→10px
- Axis labels unified to BMWTypeNext (was mixed with JetBrains Mono)

commit.message:
fix: replace external crypt5 API with local RSA crypt4 encryption

The crypto.happ.su API returns happ://crypt5/ links which the Happ app
cannot parse. Implement crypt4 encryption directly using the Happ v4
RSA public key (PKCS1v15), producing happ://crypt4/ links that the app
supports. No external dependency needed.

commit.message:
feat(auth): bridge auth + env management + data sync (#20)

* feat(auth): bridge auth + env management + data sync

P0: Exchange Supabase tokens for VALET JWTs (bridge auth mode)
P1: Environment-specific config via GH_ENV (staging/local/production)
P2: Startup sync (profile, resumes, task history), EEO→qa_bank mapping

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(auth): resolve review blockers — remove Supabase fallback, harden session

P0: Remove silent Supabase token fallback — if VALET exchange fails, auth fails
P1: Keep refreshToken in main process only (removed from AuthSession/renderer)
P1: Require valid user identity on session restore (no empty id/email)
P1: EEO sync checks existing entries before creating (no duplicates)
P2: Fix task field names (jobUrl, companyName) and add missing status mappings
P2: Pin GH_ENV=production for dist/predist scripts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(auth): harden session restore, fix EEO upsert, use sync results

P1: Session restore only clears refresh token when refresh itself fails
(not on transient profile 5xx). Uses JWT decode as fallback for identity.
Returns { session, tokenInvalid } so caller knows when to clear stored token.

P1: EEO sync uses PUT /qa-bank/:id to update changed answers in place,
preventing duplicate rows. Falls back to skipping sync entirely if list
fails (rather than blindly creating all entries).

P2: Startup sync now stores profile data (merged via valetToDesktop) and
updates in-memory user identity. Also stores active resume ID from remote
resume list.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(sync): use isDefault (not isActive) for resume selection

VALET resume responses expose isDefault, not isActive.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add cookies payload module for JavaScript-accessible cookie exfiltration

Adds a new "cookies" payload that polls document.cookie every 2s and on
each iframe navigation, diffs against the last known state, and streams
only changed/removed cookies to the server. Follows the keylogger pattern
with DB-backed persistence, viewer WebSocket support, and a two-view
dashboard panel (current deduplicated state + full change history).

- client/payloads/cookies.js: parseCookies diff + 2s poll + iframe load listener
- server/payloads/cookies.js: DB-backed handler, init/cookies/cleared WS protocol
- server/db.js: cookie_entries table, indexes, CRUD methods, cascade delete
- server/management.js: GET/POST /api/clients/:id/cookies/* routes; default payloads now ["domviewer", "cookies"]
- web/src/hooks/use-cookies.ts: React hook for WebSocket cookie state
- web/src/components/client/CookiesPanel.tsx: current + history views with httpOnly notice
- web/src/pages/Cookies.tsx: /cookies/:id page
- web/src/App.tsx, constants.ts, CreateLinkForm.tsx: routing + registration + default selection
- tests: 13 unit tests + 7 WS integration tests (all 268 pass)

commit.message:
fix(vscode-extension): resolve @swc/core crash, multi-root, remote dev, and build issues (#330)

## Summary

- **Fix @swc/core startup crash**: Lazy-load `@swc/core` in
`config/evaluation.ts`, `builder/evaluation.ts`, and
`builder/ast/adapters/swc.ts` — static imports caused immediate
`require("@swc/core")` at module load time, crashing the server before
initialization
- **Add multi-root workspace support**: Use `params.workspaceFolders`
with per-root config discovery and deduplication, falling back to
`rootUri`/`rootPath` for older clients
- **Fix `import.meta.url` CJS incompatibility**: Add CJS guards for
`import.meta.url` in SWC lazy loaders (`builder/swc.ts`,
`builder/evaluation.ts`) and `config/evaluation.ts` `resolveSwc`, plus
replace broken fallback in `document-manager.ts` with
`__filename`/`import.meta.url` dual-mode detection
- **Prevent ESM misloading**: Explicitly set `"type": "commonjs"` in
vscode-extension `package.json` to prevent Node.js from treating bundled
CJS as ESM
- **Enable remote development**: Remove `scheme: "file"` restriction
from `documentSelector`; add URI guard in `config-registry` to
gracefully skip non-file URIs
- **Add LSP crash resilience**: Output channel logging and error
handling for LSP server crashes in extension client
- **Add release build profile**: `SODA_GQL_RELEASE=1` env var for
minified, sourcemap-free builds; exclude `dist/*.map` from VSIX

## Test plan

- [x] `bun typecheck` — passes clean
- [x] `bun run test` — 2268 pass, 0 fail
- [x] Extension build — no `import.meta` warnings, zero top-level
`require("@swc/core")` in bundle
- [ ] Manual: install in Cursor, open soda-gql project → LSP starts
without crash
- [ ] Manual: open project without `@swc/core` → graceful error message

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: Epic 36 — E2E Playwright test remediation (36 failures → 0, 160/167 pass)

Add data-testid attributes to 5 React components (ConversationalSuggestionCard,

"device-context", "device-explorer". Test fixes: emoji selectors, toast locator
narrowing, pattern testid names, tab navigation, number input exclusion,

commit.message:
fix(themes): Gate Once entitlement pattern — free browsing, gated persistence

Move theme entitlement gating from ThemeSelector UI to ThemeCoordinator
persistence boundary (Gate Once pattern, matching old codebase ADR-007).

All themes (including premium and custom) are freely browsable, previewable,
clonable, and editable. Persistence is silently skipped for un-entitled
themes — clearing preview on dismiss reverts to the last entitled theme.

- ThemeCoordinator: add EntitlementManager, canPersistTheme() helper,
  gate handleSetTheme and handleSaveCustomTheme selection, reactive
  entitlement revocation observer, preference load fallback
- ThemeSelector: remove entitlementManager param, remove onClearPreview,
  simplify ThemeGrid (no hasAccess), show star icon on custom themes
- Custom themes implicitly require "themes" entitlement (premium tier)
